questions.question: Kotlin is fully interoperable with Java.
questions.explanation: You can call Java code from Kotlin and vice versa seamlessly.

questions.question: By default, all classes in Kotlin are 'open' (can be inherited from).
questions.explanation: In Kotlin, classes are 'final' by default. You must use the 'open' keyword to allow inheritance.

questions.question: The '!!' operator (double bang) is used to safely unwrap a nullable variable.
questions.explanation: The '!!' operator converts a value to a non-null type and throws an exception if the value is null. It is NOT safe.

questions.question: Kotlin lists created with 'listOf()' are mutable.
questions.explanation: 'listOf()' creates a read-only list. Use 'mutableListOf()' for a list you can change.

questions.question: Higher-Order Functions can take other functions as parameters.
questions.explanation: This is a key feature of functional programming in Kotlin.

questions.question: The 'init' block is used to initialize properties upon object creation.
questions.explanation: It runs immediately after the primary constructor.
